title: Quantum eMotion’s SecureKey Module Enters NIST Testing for FIPS 140-3 Validation - Quantum Computing Report
gist: Quantum eMotion's SecureKey cryptographic module — built on its acquired SKV Technology and paired with the Sentry-Q electron-tunneling QRNG — entered NIST's CMVP as an 'Implementation Under Test,' with the module expected to reach 'Modules in Process' status within three to four months en route to mandatory FIPS 140-3 certification.

themes.theme: Memory-less key protection racing toward FIPS 140-3

insights.claim: Quantum eMotion's SecureKey Cryptographic Module has been designated an 'Implementation Under Test' (IUT) on NIST's CMVP registry, evaluated with testing firm Intertek Laboratories, and the company expects it to advance to 'Modules in Process' (MIP) status within three to four months toward full FIPS 140-3 certification.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Gives investors a concrete near-term timeline (3-4 months) to track for the next regulatory milestone in Quantum eMotion's FIPS 140-3 certification path, which the article frames as a mandatory prerequisite for federal, financial, and healthcare sector sales.
insights.quote: The team expects the module to transition to the CMVP “Modules in Process” (MIP) status within the next three to four months.

insights.claim: Quantum eMotion acquired California-based SKV Technology Inc. to obtain the SecureKey platform, which uses a 'memory-less' cryptographic design that prevents unencrypted keys from lingering exposed in system RAM, addressing side-channel attacks, memory-scraping malware, and cold-boot exploits.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: The M&A move (acquiring SKV Technology) to build out a memory-less key protection product line shows Quantum eMotion diversifying beyond its core QRNG hardware into full-stack cryptographic execution security — relevant for assessing its product roadmap and revenue diversification.
insights.quote: Quantum eMotion acquired California-based SKV Technology Inc. to integrate the SecureKey platform into its product portfolio to address this specific vulnerability
insights.locator: Mitigating Key Exposure via Memory-Less Cryptographic Design

insights.claim: SecureKey works alongside Quantum eMotion's Sentry-Q orchestration platform, which uses a patented electron-tunneling Quantum Random Number Generator (QRNG) to generate high-entropy master keys, with SecureKey acting as the execution-bound enforcement layer.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Positions Quantum eMotion's QRNG hardware (Sentry-Q) and its new SecureKey memory-less enforcement layer as a bundled, vertically integrated key-generation-to-execution product, a differentiation angle versus vendors offering QRNG or key protection separately.
insights.quote: The company’s Sentry-Q orchestration platform uses a patented electron-tunneling Quantum Random Number Generator (QRNG) to generate high-entropy master keys. SecureKey then acts as the execution-bound enforcement layer
